rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they describe interface behavior, and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes; avoid introducing package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for interface contracts, implementations for binding details.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE annexes are declared or visible in the corresponding AADL component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within AADL component type or implementation definitions.
Component type contracts should use visible ports and features; implementation contracts may include subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific assignments or subcomponent references in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex structure (covered by KSYN-1)
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns (covered by KDEF_CARDS)
Requirement semantics and traceability (covered by requirement_analyst)
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure (covered by KSYN-3)
